metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the function (e.g. a `_stamp_and_filter_carryover(verdict, novelty_map, t_novel, carried_from_key)` step within `region_gated_floor`) that (a) iterates kept findings against the novelty_map, stamps `carried_from` on those below the novelty threshold, and (b) filters them out of the list fed to `code_review_coach_inputs`, with the workflow YAML change identified.
findings.finding: The mechanism for stamping `carried_from` on kept carryover findings and filtering them from coaching is under-specified: the plan requires carryover findings (low novelty, NOT dropped) to receive a `carried_from` field AND be omitted from coaching, but neither the Design nor the Success Criteria section identifies which function performs the novelty-map scan over kept findings to stamp `carried_from`, nor how those findings are excluded from the `surfaced` list fed to `code_review_coach_inputs` (which currently accepts all surfaced advisory findings verbatim).
findings.location: Design section ('Carryover findings carry an explicit schema field carried_from') and AC lines 'Carryover findings are stamped with the carried_from field' / 'Carryover findings are omitted from coaching while remaining surfaced'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Design section, after the `region_gated_floor` description, add: 'After computing the drop set, region_gated_floor also iterates the kept advisory findings: any finding with novelty < t_novel (i.e. scoring as carryover) is stamped {carried_from: <prior_run_key>}. The workflow step `code_review_coach_inputs` receives a pre-filtered surfaced list excluding carried_from findings (via a new `exclude_carryover=True` flag or a separate filter step); carried_from findings remain in the advisory bucket of the verdict.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `code_review_novelty.md` declares `outputs: code_review_novelty` (a gate-specific alias, mirroring `plan_review_novelty`) or `outputs: novelty` (the kernel contract), and make the `contracts.py` registration consistent with that choice.
findings.finding: The plan specifies `code_review_novelty` as the output_schema to register at `code_review/contracts.py::register_contracts()`, but simultaneously says the new prompt 'binds outputs: novelty (the reused kernel contract)'. These are contradictory: if the prompt declares `outputs: novelty`, the kernel contract fires (not `code_review_novelty`); if the prompt declares `outputs: code_review_novelty`, that is a new contract distinct from the kernel's. An executor implementing both ACs simultaneously will produce a broken prompt-to-contract binding at runtime.
findings.location: Design section: 'code_review_novelty.md ... binds outputs: novelty (the reused kernel contract)' and 'registered for code review at code_review/contracts.py::register_contracts() (importing novelty_model from review_kernel.verify)'
findings.suggested_fix: Follow the `plan_review_novelty` precedent: declare `outputs: code_review_novelty` in the prompt front-matter, and register `contracts.register_contract('code_review_novelty', novelty_model)` in `code_review/contracts.py::register_contracts()`. Remove the contradictory phrase 'binds outputs: novelty (the reused kernel contract)' — the kernel contract is reused via the same factory, but registered under a gate-specific name.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the mechanism for omitting `carried_from` findings from coaching: either (a) filter them out before the Pass-4 coaching sub-call (the deterministic pre-filter approach, analogous to how dropped findings never enter coaching), or (b) note they are passed to the coach prompt with a structural marker the coach skips.
findings.finding: The plan requires carryover findings to be 'OMITTED from coaching (not re-coached); they remain surfaced' (AC line: 'Carryover findings are omitted from coaching while remaining surfaced'), but provides no processing protocol specifying HOW the code-review coaching pass (Pass-4, `code-review-coach.md`) is made aware of `carried_from` findings and instructed to skip them. The plan names the field (`carried_from`) but not the mechanism (pre-filter before Pass-4, prompt injection of `carried_from` ids, or post-hoc dedup).
findings.location: Design section: 'Carryover findings carry an explicit schema field carried_from ... are OMITTED from coaching (not re-coached); they remain surfaced.' and corresponding AC.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Design: 'Before the Pass-4 coaching sub-call, filter out findings where carried_from is set (a deterministic pre-filter, not a prompt instruction); they are already surfaced for the client but need no new coaching note.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify `code_review_novelty.md`'s injection mechanism: either remove `{{prior_findings}}` from the template-var spec and document that prior findings are injected programmatically via `run_chunk(instructions, context)` (matching the `plan_review_novelty.md` pattern), or name the specific `prompts.resolve_prompt` call-site change that would support two substitution keys.
findings.finding: The plan specifies `code_review_novelty.md` receives `{{diff}}` + `{{prior_findings}}` as template variables, but the established runner wiring for the analogous `plan_review_novelty.md` does NOT inject prior findings as a template variable — prior findings reach the novelty sub-call programmatically via `run_chunk(instructions, context)` where context is built by `prior_findings_block()` and appended inline. The `_resolve_system` path only substitutes one plan-text variable (`{'plan': plan}`). If `{{prior_findings}}` is declared as a YAML-front-matter template var on the new prompt but the runner only resolves `{{diff}}`, the variable will be left as a literal string in the rendered prompt.
findings.location: Design section — 'Prompt spec: code_review_novelty.md receives {{diff}} + {{prior_findings}} (the surfaced prior-finding list)'
findings.suggested_fix: Follow the existing pattern: `code_review_novelty.md` should declare only `{{diff}}` (analogous to `{{plan}}`), injected as the system prompt via `_resolve_system`-equivalent; prior findings should be appended to instructions inside `run_chunk` exactly as `plan_review/__init__.py:159` does — not as a YAML template var.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an import-guard test (or linting rule) asserting no code_review module imports dso cycle machinery (arbiter, relation taxonomy, oscillation loop).
findings.finding: Parent Success Criterion 4 ('Achieved WITHOUT importing dso's heavy cycle machinery') has no child AC or test that positively asserts this negative constraint — it is structurally uncovered by all five children.
findings.location: Parent SC4; no child
findings.suggested_fix: In e51b, add a test: `import ast; assert no dso.* import in code_review/**/*.py` (or a pytest conftest import-graph assertion) and include it in the AC list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `drop_reason` vocabulary for code-review findings (at minimum `{"novelty-region", null}`) and specify that `build_payload` gains a `dropped` bucket (analogous to plan-review) so novelty-region drops are durable in the corpus.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new `drop_reason` value `"novelty-region"` for findings dropped by `region_gated_floor`, but the existing `drop_reason` enum vocabulary used in the code-review sidecar is undefined (the plan-review gate uses `{"novelty", "completion", null}`). The plan does not specify whether `"novelty-region"` extends a shared closed vocabulary or is a code-review-specific value, nor where this enum is declared for the code-review sidecar schema.
findings.location: Design section: 'Dropped novel-on-unchanged findings move to the verdict dropped bucket with drop_reason "novelty-region"'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the final two ACs whether c639 and old-frilly-plankton are (a) prerequisites that must be merged before this work lands, (b) parallel work tracked here for coordination, or (c) in-scope implementation tasks for this executor — and briefly describe each item's content so an executor can verify completion without looking up an external tracker.
findings.finding: The last two ACs ('c639 makes the code-review reader surfaced-only' and 'Bug old-frilly-plankton makes the plan-review novelty prior set surfaced-only') reference external work items by opaque identifiers without stating whether the executor of this plan is responsible for implementing them or merely verifying their completion. An executing agent cannot determine the scope boundary.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — final two bullet lines
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite as: '[ ] PREREQUISITE (c639): The code-review artifact reader filters to surfaced-only findings before loading — verify c639 is merged or implement it as part of this epic.' and '[ ] PREREQUISITE (old-frilly-plankton): The plan-review novelty prior set is filtered to surfaced-only — verify old-frilly-plankton is merged or track it as a hard dependency of this work.'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `code_review_novelty.md` must label each sub-question with its exact schema key (e.g. `cites_prior_location — ...`) alongside any rephrased wording, exactly as `plan_review_novelty.md` does, so the model emits the correct field name.
findings.finding: The new `code_review_novelty.md` prompt is specified to 'adapt the cites_prior_location sub-question wording to "same source file/line region as that prior finding?"', but the `novelty_model` schema derives its output field names directly from `NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS` (the fixed tuple `('restates_prior_defect', 'cites_prior_location', 'matches_prior_fix')`). The plan does not specify that the prompt body must explicitly anchor the rephrased question to the field key `cites_prior_location`, creating an instruction-locality gap where a model prompted with rewording may emit a different key (e.g. `cites_prior_region`) and silently degrade the answer to 0.0 (carryover — never a drop).
findings.location: Design section: 'adapts the cites_prior_location sub-question wording to "same source file/line region as that prior finding?"'
findings.suggested_fix: In the `code_review_novelty.md` prompt spec, include a note: the bullet for the location sub-question must read `- cites_prior_location — does it cite the SAME source file/line region as that prior finding?` keeping the field key as the bullet label (matching the plan_review_novelty.md pattern).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a cross-child integration test (or shared constant for the title format string) that both 3bff's emit path and c639's reader import, guaranteeing the emitted title is resolvable by the reader.
findings.finding: The artifact title string ('code-review: session:{session_id}') is defined in c639's reader (title-prefix resolution) but SET by 3bff's emit path — split ownership with no integration test spanning both children to verify the strings match.
findings.location: child c639-4122-58d9-4dc2 AC ('Local artifacts are titled code-review: session:{session_id}; the reader resolves by title prefix'); child 3bff-cdf6-8fca-4c33 (scope: sidecar.emit path)
findings.suggested_fix: Define a module-level constant `LOCAL_REVIEW_TITLE_PREFIX = 'code-review: session:'` in code_review/sidecar.py (c639's scope), import it in both the emit path (3bff) and the reader (c639), and add one integration-level test that round-trips emit→resolve.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for the `code_review_result_v1` schema extension (e.g., bump to v2, or document that extra fields are ignored by all consumers and describe the revert procedure for the artifact writer).
findings.finding: The `code_review_result_v1` payload schema is extended with new required fields (session_id, deps map) with no schema version bump and no explicit rollback/back-out plan. The plan handles old-artifact→new-reader compat (REGION_UNKNOWN fallback), but does not address the reverse: if the epic is reverted, new-format artifacts already persisted in the durable git-shared store would be read by a rolled-back reader that doesn't know these fields.
findings.location: Design section — 'Keying + addressing' and AC lines 'The code_review_result_v1 payload carries session_id as a top-level field' / 'carries a {path: sha256} deps map'
findings.suggested_fix: Either bump the artifact schema to code_review_result_v2 (expand-contract: write v2, old readers ignore unknown fields, new reader handles both), or add an explicit AC stating 'all consumers treat unknown top-level fields as optional and a revert of the epic writer leaves existing artifacts inert'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the explicit interface (function parameter or context object) by which the session_id resolved in 3bff is passed into build_payload in c639, and add an integration AC that asserts the emitted artifact's session_id field matches the title key.
findings.finding: The session_id value handshake between child 3bff (which resolves the id via resolve_session_id()/uuid4) and child c639 (whose build_payload records session_id as a top-level field) is unspecified — no child pins down the interface through which the resolved id reaches build_payload.
findings.location: child 3bff-cdf6-8fca-4c33 (scope: gate_dispatch.py); child c639-4122-58d9-4dc2 (scope: sidecar.py build_payload)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to c639's AC: 'build_payload accepts session_id as an explicit parameter (not inferred internally); produce_code_review_verdict passes the caller-resolved id.' Add to 3bff's AC: 'The id passed to the title and the id passed to build_payload are the same resolved value.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a RED fixture or golden-set eval (a small labeled set of (diff, prior_findings, expected_novelty) examples) for code_review_novelty.md to validate that the adapted sub-question wording produces reliable novelty scores before full build-out.
findings.finding: The new LLM novelty prompt for code review (code_review_novelty.md) adapts the sub-question wording to 'same source file/line region as that prior finding?' — this wording change means the prompt's scoring behavior on code-review-flavored inputs is unvalidated. The plan includes a test harness for the predicate logic (region_gated_floor) but no RED fixture, eval sample, or golden-set test that exercises the prompt itself to verify novelty scores are reliable before build-out.
findings.location: Design section: 'Prompt spec: code_review_novelty.md receives {{diff}} + {{prior_findings}}'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'A fixture with ≥3 golden (diff, prior_findings) pairs — one clearly novel, one clearly stale, one borderline — verifies code_review_novelty.md scores match expected labels.' This can reuse the plan-review novelty fixture pattern if one exists.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Mitigate the `_hash_file` private-function coupling by either promoting it to a shared utility (e.g., `review_kernel/hashing.py`) or adding an explicit re-export in attest.py's public API, and note this decision in the design doc.
findings.finding: The plan imports the private `_hash_file` primitive from plan-review's attest.py into code_review/region_gate.py. The plan acknowledges this is a cross-component private-function dependency but does not mitigate it (e.g., by promoting _hash_file to a shared utility module or wrapping it behind a published interface). This creates silent coupling: a rename or refactor of attest.py's internals will silently break region_gate.py with no interface contract protecting the boundary.
findings.location: Design section: 'the private _hash_file primitive from plan-review attest.py is reused; dependency_hashes() itself is NOT'
findings.suggested_fix: Move `_hash_file` to `review_kernel/hashing.py` (or equivalent shared module) so both plan-review and code-review import from the same published location, eliminating the cross-component private-dependency.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider whether old-frilly-plankton (a plan-review bug fix) should be tracked as a sibling/standalone ticket rather than a child of this code-review epic, since it targets a different subsystem and is independently releasable.
findings.finding: old-frilly-plankton (plan-review novelty surfaced-only fix) is a bug fix in a sibling subsystem (plan_review) with a distinct reason-to-change, bundled into a code-review epic; it is independently valuable and releasable on its own.
findings.location: AC line: 'Bug old-frilly-plankton makes the plan-review novelty prior set surfaced-only' / child ticket old-frilly-plankton

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) elevate `_hash_file` to a shared location (e.g. `review_kernel/_hashing.py` or `llm/_hashing.py`) with a public name and update attest.py to import from there, or (b) add explicit positive rationale in the Design section for why the private cross-module import is preferred over extraction.
findings.finding: The plan specifies importing the private `_hash_file` symbol directly from `plan_review/attest.py` for code_review's dep collector, but provides no positive rationale for choosing cross-module private import over extracting the function to a shared location (e.g. `review_kernel/` or a new `_hashing.py` utility), creating hidden coupling between two otherwise-independent gate modules.
findings.location: Design section: 'the private _hash_file primitive from plan-review attest.py is reused'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the `_hash_file` reuse claim: either (a) promote `_hash_file` to a shared utility module (e.g., `review_kernel/hashing.py`) re-exported from both gates, or (b) confirm the plan explicitly acknowledges the cross-module private import and accepts the coupling as a conscious tradeoff.
findings.finding: The plan asserts reuse of `_hash_file`, a module-private symbol in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py`, inside the new `code_review/region_gate.py`. Confirmed from the codebase: `_hash_file` has a leading underscore, is defined at attest.py:88, and is never re-exported from the `plan_review` package. No existing import of it exists in `code_review/`. Cross-module import of a private symbol is an unverified capability assertion — Python does not enforce module-private access, but depending on a private symbol creates a fragile inter-package coupling that may break silently on refactor.
findings.location: Design section — 'the private _hash_file primitive from plan-review attest.py is reused' / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py:88

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `cites_prior_location` in the code-review novelty prompt is semantically compatible with the shared kernel scoring math, or add a code-review-specific eval case that confirms the rephrased sub-question ('same source file/line region') produces correctly calibrated yes/insufficient/no distributions against known carryover vs novel code findings.
findings.finding: The plan repurposes the shared kernel `cites_prior_location` sub-answer (defined in `NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS` as 'same plan location/section as that prior finding?') for code review by changing only the prompt wording to 'same source file/line region as that prior finding?' — while keeping the same contract field name and scoring arithmetic. This is an unverified compatibility assertion: the semantic domain of `cites_prior_location` in the plan-review context (plan sections/AC lines) is structurally different from the code-review context (file paths and line regions), and no evidence is given that the LLM will answer the rephrased question with the same fidelity the kernel's math expects.
findings.location: Design section — 'adapts the cites_prior_location sub-question wording to same source file/line region as that prior finding?' / src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py:72

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assert the post-rollback schema state explicitly: state that `code_review_result_v1` artifacts written with the new `session_id` / deps-map fields are safely ignored (not misread) by the pre-epic reader, so rollback leaves no schema inconsistency.
findings.finding: The plan never explicitly asserts the schema state of already-written artifacts AFTER a rollback of this epic. The rubric requires the plan to assert what the schema looks like post-rollback, not just that a rollback is safe. Artifacts written during the migration window will carry the new `session_id` and deps-map fields; the plan is silent on whether the rolled-back reader handles these gracefully (ignores extra fields) or fails.
findings.location: Design section — 'Fail-safe' paragraph and Acceptance Criteria (no rollback-state assertion anywhere)
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Design 'Fail-safe' paragraph: 'Post-rollback, any artifact already written with the new fields is forward-compatible with the pre-epic reader: extra top-level fields (`session_id`, deps map) are ignored during deserialization; no data loss or mis-keying results.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either move `_hash_file` to `review_kernel` (or a shared utility module) as a public function and update both callers, or add a public re-export alias in `plan_review/attest.py` and document the coupling — do not import a `_`-prefixed symbol from a different subpackage in production code.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'reuse the private `_hash_file` primitive from plan-review attest.py' in the new `code_review/region_gate.py`, but `_hash_file` is a module-private symbol (`_`-prefixed) in a different subpackage (`plan_review`). The plan names no refactoring step (e.g., move to `review_kernel` as a shared utility, or add a public alias). Cross-subpackage import of a `_`-prefixed name in production code establishes an undocumented coupling that can silently break on internal refactors of `attest.py`.
findings.location: Design section — 'the private _hash_file primitive from plan-review attest.py is reused; dependency_hashes() itself is NOT'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rewrite the c639 and old-frilly-plankton ACs as observable end-states producible by this plan (e.g., 'The code-review reader returns only findings with decision in {block, advisory}' and 'The plan-review novelty seam filters its prior set to surfaced-only decisions before scoring'), so they are evaluable from the resulting PR diff alone.
findings.finding: The two ACs referencing c639 and old-frilly-plankton are not measurable outcomes of THIS plan's implementation. They describe what external work items do, making them non-evaluable from this plan's own repo artifacts — an executor finishing all other ACs cannot confirm these two by inspecting the work produced by this epic.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — final two bullet lines
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'c639 makes the code-review reader surfaced-only' with 'The code-review prior-finding reader filters the loaded artifact to only findings with decision in {block, advisory} before returning them to the novelty seam.' Replace 'Bug old-frilly-plankton makes the plan-review novelty prior set surfaced-only' with a note that this is a tracked dependency (not an AC of this epic) and link it in a Dependencies section instead.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the opaque 'c639' reference in the final two ACs with an inline description of the work (e.g., the file path and the behavior change) so each AC is self-contained and verifiable.
findings.finding: AC 'c639 makes the code-review reader surfaced-only' references 'c639' — an identifier that appears nowhere else in the plan body (no definition, no description, no mapping to any described work). This AC is an orphan: it cannot be implemented or verified without external context.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — '[ ] c639 makes the code-review reader surfaced-only'
findings.suggested_fix: Expand the AC to: '[ ] The code-review artifact reader filters prior findings to decision ∈ {block, advisory} (surfaced-only) before novelty scoring, analogous to the plan-review change tracked as c639.' Alternatively, define c639 in a Glossary/Terminology section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the old-frilly-plankton status: if it is in-scope work for this epic, update the Context to say 'plan-review surfaced-only is being corrected in this epic (old-frilly-plankton)'; if it is genuinely prior work that is already merged, convert the final AC to a verification note rather than a deliverable checkbox.
findings.finding: The Context section presents old-frilly-plankton as settled prior work ('plan-review side is old-frilly-plankton'), yet the final AC treats it as in-scope deliverable work ('Bug old-frilly-plankton makes the plan-review novelty prior set surfaced-only'). This is a cross-section contradiction about the completion state of old-frilly-plankton.
findings.location: Context section ('plan-review side is old-frilly-plankton') vs final AC ('[ ] Bug old-frilly-plankton makes the plan-review novelty prior set surfaced-only')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC: 'The code_review novelty contract (novelty_model / NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS) is registered in code_review/contracts.py, and a RunRequest(output_schema="code_review_novelty") succeeds without error.'
findings.finding: The Design describes code_review/contracts.py::register_contracts() as a load-bearing deliverable ('without it a RunRequest(output_schema=\'code_review_novelty\') fails at runtime'), yet no AC verifies that this contract registration exists or is correct. This is a described deliverable with no covering criterion.
findings.location: Design section — 'The reused novelty contract is registered for code review at code_review/contracts.py::register_contracts()...; without it a RunRequest(output_schema=\'code_review_novelty\') fails at runtime'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC: 'code_review_novelty.md exists with template vars {{diff}} and {{prior_findings}}, execution_mode: single_turn, category: code-review-pass, and a reviewer id of code-novelty registered in the reviewer registry.'
findings.finding: The Design specifies code_review_novelty.md in meaningful detail (template vars {{diff}} + {{prior_findings}}, execution_mode: single_turn, category: code-review-pass, adapted sub-question wording), yet no AC covers the creation or spec compliance of this prompt file. This is a described deliverable with no covering criterion.
findings.location: Design section — 'code review adds its own novelty reviewer prompt (code_review_novelty.md, oriented to a diff + a finding list)...binds outputs: novelty...adapts the cites_prior_location sub-question wording...'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a new ADR (e.g., ADR 0009) as an explicit deliverable covering the code-review memory architecture: region-gate design, keyspace isolation rationale, novelty-reuse boundary, and the uuid fallback tradeoff.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a significant new architectural decision — cross-patchset code-review finding memory with a region gate, new reviewer id (code-novelty), new prompt contract, and new keying scheme — comparable in scope to the plan-review memory covered by ADR 0008, yet no new ADR is called out as a deliverable.
findings.location: Design section (overall); ADR 0008 reference; AC list (no ADR AC present)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `docs/session-id-shims.md` is a pre-existing file or a new deliverable of this epic; if new, add an AC for its creation covering the REBAR_SESSION_ID shim pattern.
findings.finding: `docs/session-id-shims.md` is referenced in the Design section as existing documentation for harnesses lacking a native session variable, but it is not listed as an AC deliverable and may be new work introduced by this epic.
findings.location: Design section — 'docs/session-id-shims.md for harnesses lacking a native var'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a task to create or update an ADR documenting the region-gated novelty floor pattern for code review (key decisions: per-gate prompt, file-level content hashing, REGION_UNKNOWN fail-open, uuid-fallback inertness, surfaced-only prior set).
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural decision — region-gated novelty floor for code review, adapting ADR 0008's plan-review pattern to a new gate type with different keying, prompting, and region semantics — but does not mention updating ADR 0008 or creating a new ADR to document it. This is a new pipeline stage crossing multiple components.
findings.location: Design section overall; Success Criteria; no ADR update mentioned anywhere in the plan

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge existing consumers of `code_review_result_v1` and explicitly state the backward-compatibility strategy (e.g., additive-only fields with `extra='allow'`, a schema version bump, or confirmation that no strict-schema readers exist today).
findings.finding: The plan adds two new fields (session_id, deps map) to the code_review_result_v1 payload but does not acknowledge existing readers/consumers of that schema or confirm that the change is backward-compatible.
findings.location: Design section — 'Carryover findings carry an explicit schema field carried_from'; AC lines: 'The code_review_result_v1 payload carries session_id as a top-level field' and 'The code_review_result_v1 payload carries a {path: sha256} deps map'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line note in the Design section: 'code_review_result_v1 uses additive-only schema evolution; new fields (session_id, deps, carried_from) have defaults (None / {}) so existing strict-schema readers are unaffected, or a v2 alias is introduced if strict readers exist.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sentence to the Design section explicitly reconciling `region_gated_floor`'s REGION_UNCHANGED conjunct with ADR 0008's rejection of 'per-diff-region scoping' — specifically, state that the recall concern does not apply here because Pass-1 still runs the full criteria set unanchored (REGION_UNCHANGED gates only the *drop* of novel low-priority findings after full-recall Pass-1, not the criteria set run).
findings.finding: The plan's `region_gated_floor` is structurally the 'per-diff-region scoping' approach that ADR 0008 explicitly placed in 'Alternatives Rejected' — adding a region-membership conjunct to the drop predicate. ADR 0008's rationale for rejecting it was that it 'would MISS a high-stakes defect an edit introduces outside the diffed region.' The plan does not reconcile this tension: in code review, Pass-1 is still unanchored (full recall is preserved), so the ADR's recall concern may be moot here — but that reconciliation is absent from the plan text, leaving an unresolved apparent contradiction with settled prior art.
findings.location: Design section — 'A new region_gated_floor (code_review-specific) wraps rising_floor_drop with a third conjunct' / ADR 0008 Alternatives Rejected: 'Per-diff-region scoping'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Design section under the region gate description: 'This is NOT the per-diff-region scoping ADR 0008 rejected: Pass-1 still runs the full criteria set with no anchoring (full recall is preserved). The region gate only adds a drop-eligibility conjunct in Pass-3 — it never reduces the set of criteria executed or the findings surfaced for high-priority or genuinely novel findings. ADR 0008 rejected region-scoping because it reduced recall; this gate does not.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend c3e4 AC to commit to patching attest.py:441 (and any other discovered caller) to enforce surfaced-only filtering if the audit confirms the violation, not merely to 'decide' the approach.
findings.finding: Child c3e4 audits attest.py:441 for the same surfaced-only bug but does not commit to fixing it — leaving a potential unfixed caller of latest_review_result that feeds dropped findings as novelty priors.
findings.location: child c3e4-7a6c-a4cc-4fb8 AC: 'Audit every novelty prior-set consumer (attest.py:441 also calls latest_review_result) to confirm none feeds dropped findings as novelty priors.'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the AC from 'Audit … to confirm none feeds dropped findings' to 'Audit … and patch any caller found to feed dropped findings; add a regression test per patched caller.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
